consumes.artifact: agents/eval-grader.md, agents/eval-analyzer.md (current project-root agent prompt templates)
scripts/lib/eval-graders.js — loadAgentDef() call sites that currently read from project-root agents/

produces.artifact: scripts/lib/eval-graders.js with loadAgentDef paths pointing at skills/arc-evaluating/agents/

requirement.title: Co-locate Eval Agent Prompt Templates Inside the Skill Folder
requirement.description: The eval-grader, eval-analyzer (renamed from eval-analyzer), and the new eval-blind-comparator agent prompt templates MUST live under skills/arc-evaluating/agents/. The harness already loads these files as prompt templates via loadAgentDef() and execCommand('claude', ...) — it does NOT invoke them as registered subagents. Migration therefore requires only: (a) physically moving the .md files, (b) updating the path arguments inside scripts/lib/eval-graders.js's loadAgentDef calls, (c) deleting the old project-root copies. No .claude-plugin/plugin.json changes are needed and the default auto-discovery of the remaining project-root agents/ directory (implementer, debugger, planner, verifier, code-reviewer, quality-reviewer, spec-reviewer, loop-operator) MUST NOT be disturbed.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: After migration, skills/arc-evaluating/agents/eval-grader.md, eval-analyzer.md, and eval-blind-comparator.md MUST exist. The old project-root paths agents/eval-grader.md and agents/eval-analyzer.md MUST NOT exist. fr-fs-001

scripts/lib/eval-graders.js MUST read the prompt templates via the new skill-folder paths. A grep for "agents/eval-grader.md" or "agents/eval-analyzer.md" in scripts/lib/ MUST return zero matches after migration. fr-fs-001

.claude-plugin/plugin.json MUST remain unchanged by this migration. Other project-root agents (implementer, debugger, etc.) MUST continue to appear in the subagent_type registry exactly as before. fr-fs-001
arc eval ab and arc eval run MUST succeed end-to-end after migration on at least one existing scenario, proving the grader prompt is loaded from the new location without regression. fr-fs-001

requirement.title: Relocate Dashboard Code Into the Skill Folder
requirement.description: scripts/eval-dashboard.js and scripts/eval-dashboard-ui.html MUST move to skills/arc-evaluating/dashboard/. The CLI entry point arc eval dashboard MUST continue to work after the move — scripts/cli.js MAY update its require() path, but the user-facing command SHALL NOT change. No duplicated copies may remain at the old scripts/ location once the migration is complete.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: After migration, arc eval dashboard MUST start the SSE server using code located in skills/arc-evaluating/dashboard/ and scripts/eval-dashboard.* MUST no longer exist on disk. fr-fs-002
After migration, existing dashboard tests (tests/scripts/eval-dashboard.test.js) MUST either be relocated alongside the dashboard code or updated to require() the new path. CI MUST pass. fr-fs-002

requirement.title: Preserve Canonical Engine Location
requirement.description: scripts/lib/eval.js, scripts/lib/eval-stats.js, and scripts/lib/eval-graders.js MUST remain at their current canonical location. They are the arcforge core engine per .claude/rules/architecture.md and MUST NOT be moved into the skill folder. Skill-owned tooling (agents, dashboard, references, scenarios) and the canonical engine intentionally occupy different layers.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: After v2 migration, scripts/lib/eval.js, eval-stats.js, and eval-graders.js MUST still exist at their original paths. No thin re-export stub or relocation symlink MAY appear at skills/arc-evaluating/scripts/. fr-fs-003
When skill-level tooling (new CLI subcommands, dashboard, agents) needs engine functions, they MUST import from scripts/lib/ — duplicating logic into the skill folder is forbidden. fr-fs-003
